name: Roasted Peppers with Mozzarella and Carameliz
recipeIngredient: 4 medium Yellow and red bell peppers
1-1/2 teaspoons Dried thyme or 1/4 teaspoon Fresh thyme
6 ounces Fresh mozzarella cheese, sliced as thin as possible
1/4 cup Coarsely chopped Flat-leaf parsley or fresh basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast the bell peppers directly over a gas flame or under the broiler as close to the heat as possible, turning occasionally until charred all over, about 5 minutes. Enclose the peppers in a paper bag and set aside for 10 minutes to steam. With a small knife, scrape off the blackened skin; remove the cores, seeds, and ribs. Rinse under running water. Slice the peppers lengthwise into 1-inch strips. (The recipe can be prepared up to this point and covered and refrigerated up to 3 days.)
2. In a small nonreactive skillet, combine the olive oil and garlic. Cover and cook over very low heat until the garlic is very tender, 7 to 10 minutes. Uncover the pan, increase the heat to moderate, and cook until browned, about 2 minutes. Stir in the vinegar and thyme (or dried thyme), and cook for 1 minute.
3. To assemble, decoratively arrange the pepper strips and mozzarella slices on 6 small plates. Spoon the caramelized garlic dressing over the peppers and cheese and sprinkle with parsley and black pepper. Serve at room temperature. (The salad can be assembled up to 2 hours before serving.)
